reasons: Lolita can reduce Clint's front-facing poke value by reflecting ranged basic attacks and projectiles.
Clint relies on repeated enhanced basic attacks after skill casts, which gives Lolita clear shield timing.

proof.summary: Clint wants to convert skill casts into long-range enhanced basic attacks, while Lolita can reflect that pressure from the shield direction.
worksBestWhen: Lolita shields before Clint's enhanced shot connects.
The team fights through objective fronts or narrow lanes.
failureCases: Clint fires from outside Lolita's shield angle.
Lolita uses shield too early and Clint waits.

reasons: Natalia can force Clint to respect bush control instead of freely poking from side angles.
Clint is vulnerable when isolated or after using Trapping Recoil.

proof.summary: Clint wants stable long-range poke windows, while Natalia threatens hidden approach routes that force him to play closer to peel.
worksBestWhen: Natalia controls bushes around gold lane or river.
Clint has already used Trapping Recoil.
failureCases: Clint stays near tank or support protection.
Natalia is revealed before reaching him.

reasons: Lancelot can approach Clint from non-linear angles and dodge predictable poke windows.
This matchup works when Lancelot times mobility after Clint uses escape or control.

proof.summary: Clint wants enemies to approach through his poke and Trapping Recoil range, while Lancelot can use mobility to bypass straight-line pressure if executed well.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot approaches after Clint uses Trapping Recoil.
Lancelot has nearby targets for mobility chaining.
failureCases: Clint lands immobilize during Lancelot's entry.
Lancelot dives without enough damage or reset path.

reasons: Saber can punish Clint before Clint gets repeated poke cycles.
Targeted airborne burst is useful when Clint's escape tool or defensive support is unavailable.

proof.summary: Clint wants to keep distance and poke repeatedly, while Saber can force a short lock-and-burst window when Clint is exposed.
worksBestWhen: Saber attacks from fog or flank angle.
Clint has used Trapping Recoil or is away from peel.
failureCases: Clint stays protected behind frontline vision.
Saber cannot burst Clint before counter-engage arrives.

reasons: Franco can punish Clint's long-range poke posture if Clint steps forward without minion or tank cover.
Hook or suppression can remove Clint's spacing before he can kite backward.

proof.summary: Clint wants safe poke spacing, while Franco can punish exposed positioning with hook or suppression if he controls vision.
worksBestWhen: Franco hides outside Clint's vision before the hook angle.
Clint is not standing behind minions or frontline.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and loses pressure.
Clint keeps enough distance to poke without entering pickoff range.
